content.full_text:
Farn Polrrrcal PnacrrcEs CorrtvrrssroN
428 J Street o Suite 620 o Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 r Fax (916) 322-0886
November 17,2004
Derek Johnson, General Manager
Isla Vista Recreation & Park District
961 Embarcadero Del Mar
Isla Vista, CA93ll7
Re:
Your Request for Informal Assistance
Our File No. l-04-244
Dear Mr. Johnson:
This letter is in response to your request on behalf of Boardmember Bryan Brown
for advice regarding the conflict-of-interest provisions ofthe Political Reform Act (the
"Act").' Since your request largely seeks general guidance, we are treating your request
as one for informal assistance.2
There is nothing in the Act that prohibits a public official, such as Boardmernber
Brown, from holding two public positions concurrently, although other laws outside the
jurisdiction of the Fair Political Practices Commission ("Commission') may restrict the
ability of a public official from holding two public offices simultaneously, if those offices
are determined to be "incompatible" offices. You are urged to check with the state
Attorney General's office or the city attorney's office of your jurisdiction to determine if
there are other laws that may be applicable in light of the facts you present. Holding
positions on both the Isla Vista Recreation and Parks District ("[VRPD') and the project
area corrmittee ('?AC')/general plan advisory committee ("GPAC') does not, in itsel{,
create a potential disquali$ing conflict of interest under the Act. (See e.g. Foster Advice
Letter, No. 4-02-125, copy enclosed.) The Commission's advice is limited to matters
arising under the Act. (Regulation 18329(b)(8xd).) The information you provided with
your request for advice pertains to advice you received from the Santa Barbara County
Counsel regarding Government Code 1090 issues. The Commission does not provide
advice on Government Code 1090 and again, we recofllmend you contact the state
Attomey General's office for further information and guidance.
I Government Code sections 81000 - g1}l4. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections
18109-18-997, of the California Code of Regulations.
2 Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the immunity provided by an opinion or
formal written advice. (Section 83 I 14; regulation 18329(c)(3), enclosed.)
:€B


File No. 04-244
Page No. 2
If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916)
322-5660
SincerelS
Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel
By:
Galena West
Counsel, Legal Division
Gwje

sections.question_synthetic: Whether a public official can hold two concurrent positions (on the Isla Vista Recreation and Parks District and a project area committee/general plan advisory committee) and whether such positions create a disqualifying conflict of interest under the Political Reform Act.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The Political Reform Act does not prohibit a public official from holding two public positions concurrently, and the specific positions described do not create a disqualifying conflict of interest under the Act. However, other state laws regarding incompatible offices may apply, and Government Code 1090 issues should be directed to the State Attorney General's office.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: No formal QUESTION and CONCLUSION section headers present; content extracted from body of informal advice letter. Document clearly marked as informal assistance, which does not provide immunity. References enclosed Foster Advice Letter No. 4-02-125 for precedent.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether a public official can hold two concurrent positions (on the Isla Vista Recreation and Parks District and a project area committee/general plan advisory committee) and whether such positions create a disqualifying conflict of interest under the Political Reform Act.

CONCLUSION: The Political Reform Act does not prohibit a public official from holding two public positions concurrently, and the specific positions described do not create a disqualifying conflict of interest under the Act. However, other state laws regarding incompatible offices may apply, and Government Code 1090 issues should be directed to the State Attorney General's office.

Since your request largely seeks general guidance, we are treating your request as one for informal assistance.2 There is nothing in the Act that prohibits a public official, such as Boardmernber Brown, from holding two public positions concurrently, although other laws outside the jurisdiction of the Fair Political Practices Commission ("Commission') may restrict the ability of a public official from holding two public offices simultaneously, if those offices are determined to be "incompatible" offices. You are urged to check with the state Attorney General's office or the city attorney's office of your jurisdiction to determine if there are other laws that may be applicable in light of the facts you present. embedding.summary: The FPPC provided informal advice to the Isla Vista Recreation & Park District regarding whether Boardmember Bryan Brown can hold two concurrent public positions without creating a conflict of interest under the Political Reform Act. The FPPC concluded that the Act itself permits concurrent positions and the specific circumstances presented do not create a disqualifying conflict, though other applicable laws may restrict such positions.
